close_reason: Style: .to_string() on Zeroizing<String> via Display is correct
description: src/lib.rs:150 calls access_token.to_string() to produce the String that AccessTokenLoginRequest needs. Zeroizing<String> derefs to String which derefs to str, and ToString uses Display. The idiomatic alternative is access_token.as_str().to_owned() or (**access_token).clone() — both bypass Display and are slightly cheaper. The current form works because Zeroizing<String>'s Display impl forwards to String's Display, which writes the contents verbatim. Filed as opinion because the difference is negligible at runtime and Display-via-to_string on a String-ish type is widely accepted Rust. Pair with the deeper fix in finding #6 — if we drop access_token after first use, this whole conversion goes away.
title: access_token.to_string() on Zeroizing<String> goes through Display
